title: Definition: linear in θ
text: A model is linear in θ if it can be written as y_hat=θ_0+θ_1*φ_1(x)+θ_2*φ_2(x)+... where the $$\Phi_j$$ (x) are functions of $$x$$ only (no θ inside them).

title: Common non-linear-in-θ patterns
text:
Not linear in θ if: parameters are multiplied together:
$$	heta_1\cdot	heta_2$$
raised to powers:
$$(	heta_2)**{2}$$
or appear inside non-linear functions such as sin(θ₁) or exp(θ).

title: Apply to this model
text: Check each term: does any term contain θ inside a non-linear function, power, or product with another θ? If yes → not linear in θ.
